name: Conservis Farm Management API
what_it_does: Conservis farm management REST API for field operations planning, crop tracking, and agricultural business intelligence. Enables AI agents to manage field activity tracking and crop production records for farm operations automation, handle input application records (fertilizer, pesticide, seed) for agronomic compliance, access harvest yield data and field performance analytics for crop benchmarking, retrieve equipment and labor management data for farm workforce coordination, manage grain marketing and inventory tracking for commodity sales automation, handle compliance record keeping for USDA program eligibility, access multi-farm enterprise analytics for agricultural portfolio management, retrieve planting and harvest planning data for seasonal operations scheduling, manage agronomic advisor data integration for crop decision support, and integrate Conservis with precision agriculture platforms, ERP, and grain marketing systems.
use_cases: Recording field activities from farm operations agents
Tracking crop inputs from agronomic compliance agents
Analyzing yield performance from crop analytics agents
Managing grain inventory from commodity marketing agents
not_for: Backyard or hobby gardening without commercial scale
Livestock operations without crop production component
best_when: A commercial grain or row crop farm, agricultural enterprise, or crop consulting firm wanting AI agents to automate field activity recording, input compliance documentation, yield analytics, and grain marketing coordination.
avoid_when: USDA program compliance records — automated field activity and input application records submitted for USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) crop insurance or ARC/PLC program eligibility must be accurate; automated recordkeeping errors create program payment risk and potential fraud liability. Pesticide application records — automated input application records must comply with EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) and state pesticide recordkeeping requirements; automated records with incorrect rates, dates, or restricted entry intervals (REI) create state compliance risk. Crop insurance accuracy — automated yield records used for crop insurance loss claims must match FSA acreage reports; discrepancies between automated farm records and FSA/RMA data create claim denial risk.

auth.notes: Conservis uses OAuth 2.0. REST API with JSON. Minneapolis, Minnesota HQ. Founded 2010. Private (Venture-backed). Farm management software for commercial grain and row crop operations. 1,500+ farm enterprise customers. Competes with Climate FieldView, Granular (acquired by Corteva), and Farmers Edge for farm management data.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-acre annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-acre annual subscription; enterprise farm pricing
pricing.notes: Minneapolis, Minnesota. Founded 2010. Private. 1,500+ farm customers. Commercial grain and row crop focus. Competes with Climate FieldView and Granular.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per enterprise integration

known_agent_gotchas: USDA FSA PROGRAM RECORD ACCURACY: Automated field activity and yield records used for FSA crop insurance or ARC/PLC program eligibility must match FSA acreage reports; automated recordkeeping discrepancies create program payment denial and potential fraud liability

EPA pesticide recordkeeping — automated input application records must comply with EPA Worker Protection Standard and state pesticide regs; incorrect REI, rate, or date in automated records creates state department of agriculture compliance risk
Crop insurance yield data accuracy — automated yield records used for RMA/crop insurance claims must reconcile with FSA-578 acreage reports; mismatches cause claim denial
No webhooks — farm activity completion and field event notifications require polling; no push notification for planting, application, or harvest event triggers
Seasonal API usage patterns — Conservis usage spikes dramatically during planting and harvest seasons; ensure rate limit headroom during peak agricultural calendar periods

Multi-farm enterprise data isolation — automated agents accessing multiple farm operations must respect data boundaries; inadvertent cross-farm data access creates farm data privacy and competitive intelligence risk
agent_readiness.security_notes: Farm management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Farm operations and agronomic data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Farm management. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Farm operations and crop data.
